title: Fish Tacos

overview.description: Soft tortillas filled with spiced white fish, crunchy cabbage and a fresh lime yoghurt sauce.

method: Mix the cumin, paprika, coriander, garlic powder, onion powder, salt and pepper.
Pat the fish dry and coat with the oil and spice mixture.
Mix together the yoghurt, lime juice, coriander and garlic to make the sauce.
Heat a large frying pan over a medium-high heat.
Cook the fish for 3–4 minutes per side until just cooked and it flakes easily.
Rest for 2 minutes before breaking into large flakes.
Fill each tortilla with cabbage, fish and red onion.
Drizzle over the lime yoghurt sauce.

learning.did-you-know: Modern fish tacos are strongly associated with Baja California, where freshly caught fish is often grilled or fried and served in warm tortillas.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to cook delicate white fish without drying it out and how fresh toppings balance warm spices.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A quick, healthy meal that makes excellent use of frozen white fish and homemade tortillas.
chapman-tips: Pollock is an excellent budget-friendly choice and works just as well as cod.
Don't overcook the fish—it should just flake apart.
Drying the fish before seasoning helps it brown better.
The cool yoghurt sauce balances the warm spices beautifully.
notes.batch-cooking: The sauce is best made fresh. Cooked fish can be frozen, although the texture is at its best when freshly cooked.
notes.leftovers: Excellent flaked into a salad or rice bowl the following day.
